content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 25, 1988

John Glenn
President
Bay Area Rapid Transit District Board
800 Madison Street
P.O. Box 12688
Oakland, CA 94606-2688

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-240

Dear Mr. Glenn:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of
August 23, 1988, at which time you withdrew your request for
formal written advice, and requested, instead, this confirming
letter regarding your ability to appoint BART Board Members to
a liaison committee involved with the development of an
agreement between BART and Santa Clara County.

As you are aware, the Political Reform Act prohibits any
public official from participating in a governmental decision
in which he or she has a financial interest. Because of your
business dealings with Santa Clara County, you are prohibited
from participating in decisions which would foreseeably have a
material financial effect on Santa Clara County.

The question presented is whether it is foreseeable that
appointing members to the liaison committee will have a
material financial effect on Santa Clara County. Absent any
evidence that you have attempted in any way to influence the
independent actions of the board members whom you would appoint
to the liaison committee (see Benjamin Advice Letter, No.
A-86-148, copy enclosed), and based on your description of the
operation of the liaison committee, we do not feel the
foreseeability test has been met.

If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel,

By: Lilly Spitz
Counsel, Legal Division

LS:plh
Enclosure

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

BART BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
800 Madison Street - Lake Merritt Station
P.O. Box 12688
Oakland, CA 94604-2688
Telephone (415) 464-6000


August 5, 1988


Ms. Lilly Spitz
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

JOHN GLENN
PRESIDENT

WILFRED I USSERY
VICE PRESIDENT

KEITH BERNARD
SECRETARY-TREASURER

Dear Ms.Spitz:

Thank you very sincerely for your courtesy in our
meeting of August 3, 1988, and having seen me
without my having an appointment.

I am writing to follow up on our conversation
yesterday, regarding the appointment of a Liaison
Group of BART Directors to deal with their Santa
Clara counterparts on the development of an
agreement between BART and Santa Clara County for
the extension of BART service into that County.

DIRECTORS

BARCLAY SIMPSON

NELLO BIANCO

HOWARD P ABELSON

MARGARET R PRYOH

ROBERT S ALLEN

JOHN GLENN

WILFRED I USSERY

ARLO HALE SMITH

JOHN H KIRKWOOD

The Liaison group is designated to work with their
counterparts in Santa Clara County, but has no
autonomous authority or decision making authority.
The negotiating is basically done by the staffs of
each agency. The Liaison group provides policy
guidance to the staff, and brings back a recommended
agreement to a standing committee of the BART Board;
the Engineering and Operations Committee. That
Committee in turn has no autonomous authority to
commit BART or make decisions on behalf of the
District. The Engineering committee may recommend
an agreement to the full Board of Directors, which
by majority vote alone, can make binding decisions
or agreements between BART and other agencies.

With respect to the negotiating process, I would
point out that, while the staff may expect policy
guidance from the Liaison Group, the staff through
the General Manager reports to the Board as a whole,
and is charged with carrying out the Board's Policy
direction by a majority vote of the Board.
Individual Directors or a Committee of the Board,
may request actions by staff related to District
business within their purview, but does not have
authority to give explicit direction to the staff.

I think the above answers the three questions which
you posed to me. Before responding to me, I would
appreciate again discussing the above by telephone
with you. I will be out of town until August 15 and
will telephone you upon my return. Your courtesy
and assistance are sincerely appreciated.

Yours truly,

John

John Glenn
President

BART                    BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
                         800 Madison Street - Lake Merritt Station
                         P.O. Box 12688
                         Oakland, CA 94604-2688
                         Telephone (415) 464-5000


                                                         June 23, 1988


JOHN GLENN
PRESIDENT

WILFRED T USSERY                Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
VICE PRESIDENT                 General Counsel
                               California Fair Political Practices Commission
KEITH BERNARD                  428 J Street - Suite 800
GENERAL MANAGER                P.O. Box 807
                               Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

DIRECTORS                       Dear Ms. Griffiths:

                               Thank you very much for your courtesy and assistance in our
BARCLAY SIMPSON               two telephone conversations. I sincerely appreciate your
1ST DISTRICT                  permitting me to take part in the symbolic signing agreement
                               between BART and SamTrans.
NELLO BIANCO
2ND DISTRICT                   To confirm what I told you, I did not take part in the
                               negotiations. The vote by the eligible BART Board members
HOWARD P. ABELSON              was unanimous and the contract has been signed by the Vice
3RD DISTRICT                   President. Therefore, whatever I do now will not change
                               that agreement.
MARGARET K. PRYOR
4TH DISTRICT                   Pursuant to your request, I am providing the details for my
                               request to appoint a BART Board Committee to bring to the
ROBERT S. ALLEN                Board any agreement which could be made with the Santa Clara
5TH DISTRICT                   County Transportation Agency for them to join the BART
                               system. As I advised you, a firm which I own bid on a
JOHN GLENN                     contract to service the liability claims of the Agency and
PRESIDENT                      won the contract as the low bidder.

WILFRED T USSERY
VICE PRESIDENT                 The BART President is authorized to appoint all Board
                               Committees. The actual negotiations are conducted by the
ARLO HALE SMITH                BART staff. The staff has worked out a formula in which any
8TH DISTRICT                   agency joining the system must pay the total local cost of
                               what is built outside the District plus a pro rata share of
JOHN H. KIRKWOOD               what has already been constructed inside the District.
9TH DISTRICT

                               This may be somewhat confusing to you, and I would be
                               pleased to come to Sacramento with our General Manager so
                               that he can explain how the above formula is applied,
                               showing that neither I nor any other BART Director would
                               have any control over changing that formula. All Board
                               Directors are supportive of that formula.

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
Page 2
June 23, 1988

There is no way that I can personally benefit by
appointing the Board Committee for the negotiating
group. As far as my contract with Santa Clara County
Transportation Agency, it is simply awarded to the
lowest bidder and were I not to be the lowest bidder
the next time the contract is let, I would lose it
whether or not I appointed the Committee.

I respectfully request that as the unanimously elected
BART President I be permitted to appoint the Directors
to that Committee. I will not take part in the
negotiations nor any debate nor will I vote on the
matter. I will be pleased to come to Sacramento with
our General Manager to prove to you that I would have
no influence upon the outcome of any agreement that
the BART Board would accept.

Yours very truly,

[Signature]
John Glenn
President

88-2140

BART BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
800 Madison Street - Lake Merritt Station
P.O. Box 12688
Oakland, CA 94604-2668
Telephone (415) 464-6000

August 5, 1988

Ms. Lilly Spitz
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA. 95814

JOHN GLENN
PRESIDENT

WILFRED T. USSERY
VICE PRESIDENT

KEITH BERNARD
GENERAL MANAGER

Dear Ms.Spitz:

Thank you very sincerely for your courtesy in our
meeting of August 3, 1988, and having seen me
without my having an appointment.

Group of BART Directors to deal with their Santa
Clara counterparts on the development of an
agreement between BART and Santa Clara County for
the extension of BART service into that County.

DIRECTORS

BARCLAY SIMPSON
1ST DISTRICT

NELLO BIANCO
2ND DISTRICT

HOWARD P. ABELSON
3RD DISTRICT

MARGARET K. PRYOR
4TH DISTRICT

ROBERT S. ALLEN
5TH DISTRICT

JOHN GLENN
6TH DISTRICT

WILFRED T. USSERY
7TH DISTRICT

ARLO HALE SMITH
8TH DISTRICT

JOHN H. KIRKWOOD
9TH DISTRICT

The Liaison group is designated to work with their
counterparts in Santa Clara County, but has no
autonomous authority or decision making authority.
The negotiating is basically done by the staff of
each agency. The Liaison group provides policy
guidance to the staff, and brings back a recommended
agreement to a standing committee of the BART Board:
the Engineering and Operations Committee. That
Committee in turn has no autonomous authority to
commit BART or make decisions on behalf of the
District. The Engineering committee may recommend
an agreement to the full Board of Directors, which
by majority vote alone, can make binding decisions
or agreements between BART and other agencies.

With respect to the negotiating process, I would
point out that, while the staff may expect policy
guidance from the Liaison Group, the staff and the
General Manager reports to the Board as a whole,
and is charged with carrying out the Board's Policy
direction by a majority vote of the Board.
Individual Directors or a Committee of the Board,
may request actions by staff related to District
business within their purview, but does not have
authority to give explicit direction to the staff.

I think the above answers the three questions which
you posed to me. Before responding to me, I would
appreciate again discussing the above by telephone
with you. I will be out of town until August 15 and
will telephone you upon my return. Your courtesy
and assistance are sincerely appreciated.

Yours truly,

[signature]

John Glenn
President

BART                BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
                     800 Madison Street - Lake Merritt Station
                     P.O. Box 12688
                     Oakland, CA 94604-2688
                     Telephone (415) 464-6000


                                                    June 23, 1988


                     Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
                     General Counsel
JOHN GLENN           California Fair Political Practices Commission
PRESIDENT            428 J Street - Suite 800
                     P.O. Box 807
WILFRED T USSERY     Sacramento, CA  95804-0807
VICE PRESIDENT

KEITH BERNHARD       Dear Ms. Griffiths:
GENERAL MANAGER


                     Thank you very much for your courtesy and assistance in our
DIRECTORS            two telephone conversations.  I sincerely appreciate your
                     permitting me to take part in the symbolic signing agreement
BARCLAY SIMPSON      between BART and SamTrans.
1ST DISTRICT

NELLO BIANCO         To confirm what I told you, I did not take part in the
2ND DISTRICT         negotiations.  The vote by the eligible BART Board members
                     was unanimous and the contract has been signed by the Vice
HOWARD P. ABELSON    President.  Therefore, whatever I do now will not change
3RD DISTRICT         that agreement.

MARGARET K. PRYOR    Pursuant to your request, I am providing the details for my
4TH DISTRICT         request to appoint a BART Board Committee to bring to the
                     Board any agreement which could be made with the Santa Clara
ROBERT S. ALLEN      County Transportation Agency for them to join the BART
5TH DISTRICT         system.  As I advised you, a firm which I own bid on a
                     contract to service the liability claims of the Agency and
JOHN GLENN           won the contract as the low bidder.
6TH DISTRICT

WILFRED T USSERY     The BART President is authorized to appoint all Board
7TH DISTRICT         Committees.  The actual negotiations are conducted by the
                     BART staff.  The staff has worked out a formula in which any
ARLO HALE SMITH      agency joining the system must pay the total local cost of
8TH DISTRICT         what is built outside the District plus a pro rata share of
                     what has already been constructed inside the District.
JOHN H KIRKWOOD
9TH DISTRICT         This may be somewhat confusing to you, and I would be
                     pleased to come to Sacramento with our General Manager so
                     that he can explain how the above formula is applied,
                     showing that neither I nor any other BART Director would
                     have any control over changing that formula.  All Board
                     Directors are supportive of that formula.

Ms. Diane M. Griffiths
Page 2
June 23, 1988


There is no way that I can personally benefit by
appointing the Board Committee for the negotiating
group. As far as my contract with Santa Clara County
Transportation Agency, it is simply awarded to the
lowest bidder and were I not to be the lowest bidder
the next time the contract is let, I would lose it
whether or not I appointed the Committee.

I respectfully request that as the unanimously elected
BART President I be permitted to appoint the Directors
to that Committee. I will not take part in the
negotiations nor any debate nor will I vote on the
matter. I will be pleased to come to Sacramento with
our General Manager to prove to you that I would have
no influence upon the outcome of any agreement that
the BART Board would accept.


Yours very truly,

[signature]
John Glenn
President

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 30, 1988




John Glenn
Bay Area Rapid Transit District
P.O. Box 12688
Oakland, CA  94604-2688

Re:  File No. 88-240

Dear Mr. Glenn:

Your letter received by the Fair Political Practices
Commission on June 27, 1988, has been reassigned to Lilly Spitz
an attorney in the Legal Division.  If you have any questions
regarding your letter, you may reach her at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

sections.question: Whether it is foreseeable that appointing members to the liaison committee will have a material financial effect on Santa Clara County, given the requestor's business dealings with Santa Clara County.
sections.conclusion: Absent any evidence that the requestor has attempted in any way to influence the independent actions of the board members whom he would appoint to the liaison committee, and based on the description of the operation of the liaison committee, the foreseeability test has not been met. The requestor may appoint members to the liaison committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether BART President John Glenn, who owns a firm with a contract to service liability claims for Santa Clara County Transportation Agency, can appoint BART Board Members to a liaison committee involved in developing an agreement between BART and Santa Clara County without violating the Political Reform Act's prohibition on participating in decisions with foreseeable material financial effects.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the foreseeability test under the Political Reform Act is not met because there is no evidence of attempted influence over appointed board members and the liaison committee has no autonomous decision-making authority. Therefore, the requestor may appoint BART Board Members to the liaison committee despite his financial interest with Santa Clara County.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letter exchanges including incoming requests (June 23, August 5) and the formal FPPC response (August 25, 1988). The requestor withdrew his formal advice request and instead requested a confirming letter. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable from the August 25 FPPC letter.

QUESTION: Whether it is foreseeable that appointing members to the liaison committee will have a material financial effect on Santa Clara County, given the requestor's business dealings with Santa Clara County.

CONCLUSION: Absent any evidence that the requestor has attempted in any way to influence the independent actions of the board members whom he would appoint to the liaison committee, and based on the description of the operation of the liaison committee, the foreseeability test has not been met. The requestor may appoint members to the liaison committee.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to BART President John Glenn's withdrawal of a formal request, confirming via letter his ability to appoint BART Board Members to a liaison committee for BART-Santa Clara County negotiations despite his ownership of a firm with a liability claims contract with Santa Clara County Transportation Agency. The FPPC determined the foreseeability test was not met under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
